title: The amenity basket
text: Read six category ids free catalog, turn city name centre point.
code: library(closecity) library(sf)  close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here  types <- close$destination_types()$data$destination_types labels <- sapply(types, `[[`, "label") ids <- sapply(types, `[[`, "dest_type_id")  basket <- c(   grocery = ids[labels == "grocery_stores"],   library = ids[labels == "libraries"],   park = ids[labels == "parks"],   transit = ids[labels == "frequent_transit"],   restaurant = ids[labels == "restaurants"],   cafe = ids[labels == "cafes"] )  city <- close$places("Richmond")$results[[1]]

title: The amenity basket
text: One call gets walk time every block near downtown six categories, along block’s population. result sf table.
code: blocks <- close$blocks_query(   center = list(lon = city$lon, lat = city$lat), radius_m = 2500,   mode = "walk", type = unname(basket), include_population = TRUE )  # One row per block, for population and for mapping. one_per_block <- blocks[!duplicated(blocks$geoid), ] total_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population)

what: Coverage, one amenity at a time
title: The amenity basket
text: amenity, block counts covered within 15-minute walk. Add population covered blocks. Parks restaurants tend everywhere; groceries frequent transit usually hardest reach. Map one amenity see pattern.
code: for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% covered])   cat(sprintf("%-11s %3.0f%%\n", name, 100 * pop / total_pop)) } near_transit <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket["transit"] &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 15]) one_per_block$has_transit <- one_per_block$geoid %in% near_transit  plot(one_per_block["has_transit"], pal = c("#eef0f7", "#058040"), border = NA)

title: The amenity basket
text: block fully covered six amenities within 15 minutes. Start every block remove ones miss amenity.
code: covered_all <- one_per_block$geoid for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   covered_all <- intersect(covered_all, covered) }  basket_pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% covered_all]) cat(sprintf("All six amenities: %.0f%% of residents\n", 100 * basket_pop / total_pop))  one_per_block$full_basket <- one_per_block$geoid %in% covered_all plot(one_per_block["full_basket"], pal = c("#eef0f7", "#f36e21"), border = NA)

title: The amenity basket
text: Look residents yet fully covered, count many missing amenity. amenity people lack one add first. Map uncovered blocks see new amenities good.
code: uncovered <- setdiff(one_per_block$geoid, covered_all)  for (name in names(basket)) {   covered <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == basket[name] &                                    blocks$travel_time <= 15])   lacking <- setdiff(uncovered, covered)   pop <- sum(one_per_block$population[one_per_block$geoid %in% lacking])   cat(sprintf("%-11s %6.0f residents would gain access\n", name, pop)) } one_per_block$uncovered <- one_per_block$geoid %in% uncovered plot(one_per_block["uncovered"], pal = c("#eef0f7", "#202a5b"), border = NA)

title: Get started with closecity
text: terms come throughout: Census block. smallest area Census Bureau publishes. one 15-digit id, GEOID. Destination type. category place, grocery stores libraries. Every type numeric id. Mode. someone travels: walk, bike, transit. Isochrone. area reachable point within time limit, polygon. Catchment. reverse: every block can reach given place.

title: Get started with closecity
text: make every request client object. catalog lookup routes free, can also start without key:
code: library(closecity)  # The key (ck_live_ or ck_test_) comes from https://account.close.city close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here close <- close_client() close$modes()$data$modes                       # walk, bike, transit close$last_updated()$data$last_updated

what: Look things up instead of guessing
title: Get started with closecity
text: Two free calls save memorising codes. Use $destination_types() find numeric id category, $places() turn city name GEOID centre point.
code: # The catalog lists every category with its numeric id and label. types <- close$destination_types()$data$destination_types labels <- sapply(types, `[[`, "label") ids <- sapply(types, `[[`, "dest_type_id") grocery_id <- ids[labels == "grocery_stores"]  # A city name gives you a GEOID and a centre point. providence <- close$places("Providence")$results[[1]] providence$geoid

what: Make a call and map it
title: Get started with closecity
text: Feature methods return sf object, can map result straight away.
code: library(sf)  groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = providence$lat, lon = providence$lon,                                radius_m = 1500, type = grocery_id) plot(st_geometry(groceries), pch = 19, col = "#202a5b")

title: Get started with closecity
text: List routes return one page time. $records() follows pages end.
code: all_groceries <- close$records("pois_search", lat = providence$lat,                                lon = providence$lon, radius_m = 1500,                                type = grocery_id)

title: Get started with closecity
text: Set spatial = FALSE work raw data. Block routes join census-block boundaries , using tigris package download .
code: close$spatial <- FALSE summary <- close$block_summary("440070036001010", mode = "walk") summary$results

title: Get started with closecity
text: Failed requests raise classed condition. Catch base close_api_error, specific one.
code: tryCatch(   close$block_summary("000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) message(sprintf("%s (%d)", e$slug, e$status)) )

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Read cafe category id free catalog find city centre.
code: library(closecity) library(sf)  close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here  types <- close$destination_types()$data$destination_types labels <- sapply(types, `[[`, "label") ids <- sapply(types, `[[`, "dest_type_id") cafe <- ids[labels == "cafes"]  city <- close$places("Providence")$results[[1]]

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Search cafes near downtown. result sf points, plot pick one shop subject.
code: cafes <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                            radius_m = 1200, type = cafe) plot(st_geometry(cafes), pch = 19, col = "#202a5b")  ours <- cafes[1, ] ours$name

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Ask every block can reach shop within 10-minute walk. comes back sf polygons, block boundaries downloaded tigris.
code: our_shed <- close$poi_catchment(ours$dest_id, mode = "walk", max_minutes = 10)  plot(st_geometry(our_shed), col = "#eef0f7", border = "#c6cbe0") plot(st_geometry(ours), add = TRUE, pch = 19, col = "#f36e21", cex = 1.6)

title: Competitor walksheds
text: nearest competitors, pull walkshed count blocks share . our_shed$geoid just vector block ids, overlap plain set intersection.
code: for (i in 2:6) {   their_shed <- close$poi_catchment(cafes$dest_id[i], mode = "walk",                                     max_minutes = 10)   shared <- intersect(our_shed$geoid, their_shed$geoid)   cat(sprintf("%-28s %3d shared blocks (%.0f%% of ours)\n",               cafes$name[i], length(shared),               100 * length(shared) / nrow(our_shed))) }

title: Competitor walksheds
text: Draw walkshed, every cafe top, shop highlighted. clusters competitors inside walkshed ones competing walk-traffic. recipe works wider area: search bounding box instead radius, loop shops.
code: plot(st_geometry(our_shed), col = "#eef0f7", border = "#c6cbe0") plot(st_geometry(cafes), add = TRUE, pch = 19, col = "#202a5b") plot(st_geometry(ours), add = TRUE, pch = 19, col = "#f36e21", cex = 1.6)

title: Looking for a home
text: Build client, read pieces need free catalog instead memorising codes.
code: library(closecity) library(sf)  close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here  # The catalog lists every category with its numeric id. Pull the ids you need. types <- close$destination_types()$data$destination_types labels <- sapply(types, `[[`, "label") ids <- sapply(types, `[[`, "dest_type_id")  grocery <- ids[labels == "grocery_stores"] restaurant <- ids[labels == "restaurants"] transit <- ids[labels == "frequent_transit"]  # Turn the city name into a GEOID and a centre point. city <- close$places("Somerville")$results[[1]]

title: Looking for a home
text: Look raw ingredients first. search returns points, plot three amenities three layers.
code: groceries <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                                radius_m = 3000, type = grocery) restaurants <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                                  radius_m = 3000, type = restaurant) stops <- close$pois_search(lat = city$lat, lon = city$lon,                            radius_m = 3000, type = transit)  plot(st_geometry(restaurants), col = "#c6cbe0", pch = 19, cex = 0.6) plot(st_geometry(groceries), col = "#058040", pch = 19, add = TRUE) plot(st_geometry(stops), col = "#f36e21", pch = 17, add = TRUE)

title: Looking for a home
text: Pull per-block walk times whole city. result sf table one row per (block, category); block boundaries come tigris, downloaded cached. amenity rule, pick blocks pass one, keep blocks pass three.
code: blocks <- close$place_blocks(city$geoid, mode = "walk",                              type = c(grocery, restaurant, transit)) near_grocery <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == grocery &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 10]) near_restaurant <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == restaurant &                                          blocks$travel_time <= 5]) near_transit <- unique(blocks$geoid[blocks$dest_type_id == transit &                                       blocks$travel_time <= 20])  candidates <- intersect(intersect(near_grocery, near_restaurant), near_transit) winners <- blocks[blocks$geoid %in% candidates, ]  plot(st_geometry(blocks), col = "#eef0f7", border = "white") plot(st_geometry(winners), col = "#f36e21", border = NA, add = TRUE)

title: Looking for a home
text: Suppose two work different places. transit isochrone workplace shows far commute reaches. come back polygons. Keep winning blocks also sit inside commutes. short list look.
code: work_a <- close$isochrone(lon = -71.0865, lat = 42.3625, mode = "transit",                           direction = "from", minutes = 20) work_b <- close$isochrone(lon = -71.0589, lat = 42.3555, mode = "transit",                           direction = "from", minutes = 20)  plot(st_geometry(work_a), col = "#05804033", border = "#058040") plot(st_geometry(work_b), col = "#f36e2133", border = "#f36e21", add = TRUE) both_commutes <- st_intersection(st_union(work_a), st_union(work_b)) shortlist <- winners[st_intersects(winners, both_commutes, sparse = FALSE)[, 1], ]  plot(st_geometry(winners), col = "#eef0f7", border = "white") plot(st_geometry(shortlist), col = "#058040", border = NA, add = TRUE)

code: @Manual{,   title = {closecity: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)},   author = {Nathaniel Henry},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r}, }

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: R client Close API. Get travel times every US census block nearby places, foot, bike, public transit. data behind close.city, read Close API. Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("henryspatialanalysis/closecity-r")

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: make requests client object. Feature results come back sf objects, can map right away. Catalog lookup routes free need key:
code: library(closecity)  # The key (ck_live_ or ck_test_) comes from https://account.close.city close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here  # Coffee shops within a 1.5 km walk of a point, as an sf of points: cafes <- close$pois_search(lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412, radius_m = 1500, type = 31) plot(sf::st_geometry(cafes)) close <- close_client() close$modes()$data$modes                       # walk, bike, transit close$places("Providence")$results[[1]]        # a city name to its GEOID and centre types <- close$destination_types()$data$destination_types

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: terms show throughout API: Census block. smallest unit Census Bureau publishes. one 15-digit id called GEOID. Destination type. category place, grocery stores libraries. type numeric id. Look close$destination_types(). Mode. someone travels: walk, bike, transit. Isochrone. area can reach point within time limit, drawn polygon. Catchment. reverse isochrone: every block can reach given place. Token. One unit API usage. Free routes cost nothing; data routes draw monthly allowance.

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: List routes return one page time. Use $records() follow pages end return everything .
code: all_cafes <- close$records("pois_search", lat = 41.823, lon = -71.412,                            radius_m = 1500, type = 31)

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Feature methods return sf default. Block routes join census-block boundaries (needs tigris package, downloads boundaries caches ). work raw data instead, set spatial = FALSE: can also flip time close$spatial <- FALSE.
code: close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key", spatial = FALSE)   # use your own key here reply <- close$block_summary("250173523004004", mode = "walk") reply$results

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Failed requests raise classed condition. Catch base close_api_error, specific one close_api_tokens_exhausted.
code: tryCatch(   close$block_summary("000000000000000"),   close_api_error = function(e) message(sprintf("%s (%d)", e$slug, e$status)) )

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)
text: Documentation: https://henryspatialanalysis.github.io/closecity-r/ Interactive API: https://api.close.city/docs Machine-readable contract: https://api.close.city/openapi.json

title: Client for the Close API (api.close.city)

title: Close API client — CloseClient
text: R6 object holds connection settings gives one method per public route. Create close_client() rather calling $new(). Feature methods return sf object spatial TRUE (default), close_reply otherwise.

what: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: Detects geometry payload. POI replies ($pois_search(), $block_pois(), $point_pois(), $poi()) become points lat/lon. isochrone reply format = "geojson" becomes polygons. Block replies ($blocks_query(), $place_blocks(), $poi_catchment()) carry GEOIDs, block boundaries joined block_geometry, downloaded tigris fetch = TRUE.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: close_as_sf(   x,   block_geometry = NULL,   geoid_col = "GEOID20",   crs = 4326,   fetch = FALSE )

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: x close_reply (list shape). block_geometry Optional sf block boundaries geoid_col column, joined block replies 15-digit GEOID. geoid_col Name GEOID column block_geometry. Default "GEOID20" (TIGER 2020 blocks). crs Coordinate reference system point polygon geometry. Default 4326. fetch TRUE block_geometry NULL, download needed TIGER blocks tigris (inferring state county GEOIDs).

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
text: sf data frame.

title: Convert a Close reply to an sf object — close_as_sf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client(spatial = FALSE) close_as_sf(close$pois_search(lat = 41.82, lon = -71.41, radius_m = 1500)) } # }

what: Create a Close API client — close_client
title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: Builds CloseClient. catalog health routes free, key optional. Every data route needs one (ck_live_ ck_test_ key), created https://account.close.city.

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
code: close_client(   api_key = NULL,   base_url = DEFAULT_BASE_URL,   timeout = 30,   spatial = TRUE )

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: api_key API key, NULL free routes. base_url API base URL. timeout Request timeout, seconds. spatial Return feature results sf objects? Defaults TRUE. Set FALSE work raw close_reply instead.

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
text: CloseClient. Make calls methods.

title: Create a Close API client — close_client
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ close <- close_client("ck_live_your_key")   # use your own key here close$block_summary("250173523004004", mode = "walk") } # }

title: The reply object — close_reply
text: spatial FALSE, every method returns close_reply: list parsed body plus metering caching information named fields.

title: The reply object — close_reply
text: data parsed body (list), NULL 304. results results array list routes (else empty list). next_cursor cursor next page, NULL. tokens_charged, tokens_remaining Token counts; NULL free routes. etag response ETag, pass back if_none_match. status HTTP status code. not_modified TRUE 304 (data NULL). request_id Server request id, handy support.

title: closecity 1.0.0
text: First public release closecity R client Close API (api.close.city). R6 client, close_client(), one method per public route: catalog, place lookup, block point summaries POIs, POI search, detail, catchment, areal block queries, isochrones. Feature methods return sf objects default. Set spatial = FALSE work raw reply. Block boundaries joined tigris. First-class metering (tokens_charged, tokens_remaining), ETag/304 conditional requests, keyset pagination via $records(), typed RFC 9457 errors classed conditions. $places() looks city town name returns GEOID centre.
